title: The Clearing Where Both Paths Converge

features.name: two-sheds
features.desc-on-touch: you stand where the path was. the two sheds hold the moment of choice — each one keeps what you did not choose, what the earth remembers you not bringing

features.name: fence-line-merge
features.desc-on-touch: the fences that led you here have learned to meet. you touch them and feel both footsteps at once

features.name: doubled-footstep-stone
features.desc-on-touch: one footprint. two weights. the earth pressed twice in one place

features.name: harvest-basket-left
features.desc-on-touch: what was gathered along one path, set down where the other path waited. the gathering continues in both directions now

features.name: mirror-post
features.desc-on-touch: it still bends. the mirror effect fades here — the paths are becoming real, diverging into different weather

inscription: the earth keeps both memories; the clearing knows you twice

file.name: TwoPathsInOneFootprint_1987.wav
file.note: a recording made standing between two choices, or two recordings made simultaneously, the archive cannot decide which

reverie.title: REVERIE — the clearing at midday
narr: You stand where she stood, where he stood, where the
splitting happened. The earth beneath you holds four feet
pressed in the same dust. She traces the line where
the fence bends; he holds your wrist, counting the
moments where divergence hasn't yet divided you. You are
all here together, in the place that refused to choose.

exits.label: back through the bending fence to the mirrored field
toward the next field, where the paths learn separate names
into denser growth, where the clearing closes behind
alongside a ditch, toward older ground
